description_long: This cowshed is a long, rectangular agricultural building with a gable roof covered in reddish-brown corrugated material. The walls are clad in a light tan, wood-textured material, and the structure is elevated slightly above the ground. Along one of the long sides, there are regularly spaced horizontal slats or vents, likely for ventilation, and subtle vertical paneling is visible on the end walls. The roof overhangs the walls slightly, providing additional weather protection. There are no visible windows or doors in the provided views, suggesting entrances may be on the unseen sides or integrated subtly into the design.
description: A long, tan wooden cowshed with a reddish-brown gable roof and horizontal ventilation slats along one side.
description_view_0: The long side of the cowshed is visible, showing horizontal ventilation slats and the full length of the reddish-brown gable roof.
description_view_1: The gable end of the cowshed is shown, highlighting the triangular roof peak and plain wooden wall with no visible openings.
description_view_2: The opposite long side of the cowshed is visible, featuring a plain tan wall with no slats or openings and the full roof span.
description_view_3: The other gable end is shown, mirroring view 1 with a plain wooden wall and the roof peak.
attribute_door_style: not visible; likely sliding barn door or single wooden plank typical for cowsheds
attribute_ventilation_features: horizontal slatted vents along one long side
